title: Nmap Mastery Challenge
prerequisites: Lab with 3 target machines with different configs
learning_objectives: Apply advanced Nmap scanning techniques against diverse targets
Compare stealth and full scan approaches
Design custom scan strategies for firewalled targets
scenario: Your mentor has set up 3 target machines with different configurations. Complete a series of scanning challenges, each requiring different Nmap techniques. Document every command and result precisely.

scenario_context.challenge: Progressive Nmap challenges from basic to firewall evasion
scenario_context.relevance: Nmap is the most important tool in an offensive operator's arsenal. Mastering its capabilities is non-negotiable.

subtasks.title: Discovery Scan
subtasks.description: Find all live hosts using host discovery only (no port scanning). Document flags used and rationale.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use nmap -sn. Try different discovery methods: ICMP echo, TCP SYN, TCP ACK, ARP. Explain why you chose each method and what each discovers.
subtasks.evidence_required: Host discovery output and command explanation

subtasks.title: Stealth vs Full Scan Comparison
subtasks.description: Run both a SYN stealth scan and full connect scan against Host 1. Compare results, timing, and detection risk.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Run nmap -sS (SYN) and nmap -sT (connect) against the same host. Compare: results, speed, packets sent, detection likelihood. Explain when to use each.
subtasks.evidence_required: Both scan outputs plus comparison analysis

subtasks.title: Version & Script Scan
subtasks.description: Run comprehensive version detection plus default scripts against Host 2. Identify all services and script findings.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Use nmap -sV -sC or nmap -A. Analyse NSE script output. Note any vulnerabilities or misconfigurations the scripts reveal.
subtasks.evidence_required: Full scan output plus findings analysis

subtasks.title: Custom Scan Challenge
subtasks.description: Host 3 has a firewall. Design and execute a scan strategy to identify services despite the firewall. Try multiple approaches.
subtasks.detailed_guidance: Try: fragment packets (-f), decoys (-D), different timing (-T), ACK scan (-sA), window scan (-sW), specific port ranges. Document what works and what's blocked.

reflection_prompt: A client's IDS alerts on your scans within seconds. How do you adjust your approach to complete the engagement without triggering excessive alerts?

quiz.question: SYN scan is called 'stealth' because:
options: It doesn't complete the TCP handshake, making it harder to log
quiz.explanation: SYN scan sends SYN and receives SYN-ACK but never completes with ACK. Many older systems don't log incomplete connections.

quiz.question: Against a firewalled host, an ACK scan (-sA) helps by:
options: Revealing which ports are filtered vs unfiltered by the firewall
quiz.explanation: ACK scans don't determine open ports but reveal firewall rules by showing which ports are filtered vs unfiltered.

mentor_notes: Evaluate command selection rationale, not just results. The firewall evasion challenge should show creative thinking and systematic approach.
